key_points: Political institutions help in making and implementing government decisions
Institutions ensure democratic governance through procedures and accountability
The Mandal Commission decision highlighted how government decisions involve multiple institutions
The Indian Parliament performs legislative, financial and representative functions
Lok Sabha represents the people and has control over the Council of Ministers
Rajya Sabha represents the states and participates in law making
The executive includes the President, Prime Minister, Council of Ministers and civil servants
The political executive consists of elected leaders who make policy decisions
The permanent executive includes civil servants who assist in administration
The Prime Minister plays a key role in coordinating ministries and leading the Cabinet
The Cabinet is the inner group of ministers responsible for major policy decisions
The President is the constitutional head of the state
The judiciary acts as the guardian of the Constitution
The Supreme Court ensures that laws follow the Constitution
Public Interest Litigation allows courts to address issues affecting public welfare
examples: Example of democratic decision: Mandal Commission implementation
Example of executive authority: Prime Minister and Cabinet
Example of judicial authority: Supreme Court decision
Example of citizen participation: Public Interest Litigation
Example of parliamentary control: Question hour
Example of constitutional protection: Judicial review
formulas: Judiciary → Interprets laws and protects Constitution
Checks and balances → Distribution of power among institutions
exam_tips: Understand how decisions are taken through institutional procedures
Remember the difference between political executive and permanent executive
Know the powers and functions of Parliament
Understand the role of the Prime Minister and Cabinet in governance
Remember that the President acts on the advice of the Council of Ministers
Study the structure and functions of the judiciary
Understand the concept of judicial review
Public Interest Litigation allows citizens to seek justice for public causes
